items.name: select-basic
items.sql: SELECT 1

items.name: select-filter
items.sql: SELECT id, name FROM public.users WHERE id = 42

items.name: select-join
items.sql: SELECT u.id, u.name, o.order_no FROM public.users u JOIN public.orders o ON u.id = o.user_id WHERE o.status = 'paid'

items.name: select-cte
items.sql: WITH active_users AS (SELECT id, name FROM public.users WHERE status = 'active') SELECT au.id, au.name FROM active_users au

items.name: insert-single-row
items.sql: INSERT INTO public.users (id, name) VALUES (1, 'alice')

items.name: insert-multi-row
items.sql: INSERT INTO public.users (id, name) VALUES (1, 'alice'), (2, 'bob')

items.name: insert-from-select
items.sql: INSERT INTO public.user_archive (id, name) SELECT id, name FROM public.users WHERE status = 'inactive'

items.name: update-basic
items.sql: UPDATE public.users SET name = 'alice', status = 'active' WHERE id = 1

items.name: delete-conditional
items.sql: DELETE FROM public.users WHERE id = 1 AND status = 'active'

items.name: delete-in-list
items.sql: DELETE FROM public.users WHERE id IN (1, 2, 3) AND status = 'active'

items.name: drop-table
items.sql: DROP TABLE public.users

items.name: drop-view
items.sql: DROP VIEW public.v_users

items.name: create-view
items.sql: CREATE VIEW public.v_users AS SELECT id, name FROM public.users

items.name: truncate-table
items.sql: TRUNCATE TABLE public.users

items.name: comment-table
items.sql: COMMENT ON TABLE public.users IS 'hot'

items.name: rename-table
items.sql: ALTER TABLE public.users RENAME TO users_archive

items.name: alter-table-add-column
items.sql: ALTER TABLE public.users ADD COLUMN age integer

items.name: create-index
items.sql: CREATE INDEX idx_users_name ON public.users (name)

items.name: drop-index
items.sql: DROP INDEX public.idx_users_name

items.name: explain-select
items.sql: EXPLAIN SELECT id FROM public.users

items.name: copy-table
items.sql: COPY public.users (id, name) FROM STDIN

items.name: lock-table
items.sql: LOCK TABLE public.users IN ACCESS EXCLUSIVE MODE

items.name: call-procedure
items.sql: CALL public.refresh_users()

items.name: do-block
items.sql: DO $$ BEGIN NULL; END $$

items.name: create-table-as
items.sql: CREATE TABLE public.active_users AS SELECT id FROM public.users WHERE status = 'active'

items.name: transaction-begin-commit
items.sql: BEGIN; COMMIT;

items.name: transaction-begin-insert-rollback
items.sql: BEGIN; INSERT INTO public.users (id, name) VALUES (1, 'alice'); ROLLBACK;

items.name: multi-statement-mixed
items.sql: SELECT 1; INSERT INTO public.audit_log (id, action) VALUES (1, 'login')

items.name: quoted-identifiers
items.sql: SELECT "User"."Name" FROM "User"

items.name: literal-semicolon
items.sql: SELECT ';' AS token

items.name: select-subquery-exists
items.sql: SELECT u.id, EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.orders o WHERE o.user_id = u.id) AS has_orders FROM public.users u

items.name: select-case-window
items.sql: SELECT p.id, CASE WHEN p.status = 'paid' THEN p.order_no ELSE 'pending' END AS label, sum(p.amount) OVER (PARTITION BY p.user_id ORDER BY p.created_at) AS running_amount FROM public.payments p

items.name: select-union-order-limit
items.sql: SELECT id FROM public.users UNION ALL SELECT id FROM public.user_archive ORDER BY id LIMIT 10

expect.deparse_contains: UNION ALL SELECT id FROM public.user_archive

items.name: insert-on-conflict-update
items.sql: INSERT INTO public.users (id, name) VALUES (1, 'alice') ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET name = EXCLUDED.name RETURNING id

items.name: insert-returning
items.sql: INSERT INTO public.audit_log (id, action) VALUES (1, 'login') RETURNING id, action

items.name: update-from-returning
items.sql: UPDATE public.users AS u SET status = src.status FROM public.user_stage AS src WHERE u.id = src.id RETURNING u.id

items.name: delete-using-returning
items.sql: DELETE FROM public.users USING public.user_archive AS ua WHERE public.users.id = ua.id RETURNING public.users.id

items.name: merge-basic
items.sql: MERGE INTO public.target_table AS t USING public.source_table AS s ON t.id = s.id WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET name = s.name WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT (id, name) VALUES (s.id, s.name)

items.name: savepoint-release
items.sql: BEGIN; SAVEPOINT sp1; RELEASE SAVEPOINT sp1; COMMIT;

items.name: rollback-to-savepoint
items.sql: BEGIN; SAVEPOINT sp1; INSERT INTO public.audit_log (id, action) VALUES (1, 'login'); ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT sp1; COMMIT;

items.name: create-materialized-view
items.sql: CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW public.mv_users AS SELECT id, name FROM public.users

items.name: alter-table-drop-column
items.sql: ALTER TABLE public.users DROP COLUMN age

items.name: create-schema
items.sql: CREATE SCHEMA analytics

items.name: drop-schema
items.sql: DROP SCHEMA analytics

items.name: grant-select
items.sql: GRANT SELECT ON TABLE public.users TO analyst

expect.deparse_contains: GRANT select ON public.users TO analyst

items.name: revoke-select
items.sql: REVOKE SELECT ON TABLE public.users FROM analyst

expect.deparse_contains: REVOKE select ON public.users FROM analyst

items.name: analyze-table
items.sql: ANALYZE public.users

items.name: vacuum-analyze-table
items.sql: VACUUM ANALYZE public.users

items.name: postgresql-set-search-path
items.sql: SET search_path TO app_schema, public

items.name: postgresql-set-schema
items.sql: SET SCHEMA 'app_schema'

items.name: postgresql-set-local-search-path
items.sql: SET LOCAL search_path = app_schema

items.name: postgresql-prepare-select
items.sql: PREPARE user_by_id(int) AS SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = $1

items.name: postgresql-execute-prepared
items.sql: EXECUTE user_by_id(42)

items.name: postgresql-deallocate-prepare
items.sql: DEALLOCATE PREPARE user_by_id

items.name: oracle-cli-dialect-q-quote
items.sql: SELECT q'[Bob's order]' AS label FROM dual

items.name: sqlserver-cli-dialect-top-param
items.sql: SELECT TOP (3) [id], [name] FROM [dbo].[users] WHERE [id] = @id ORDER BY [id]

items.name: dameng-cli-dialect-set-schema-top
items.sql: SET SCHEMA KDES; SELECT TOP 2 id, name FROM users WHERE id = :id ORDER BY id

items.name: postgresql-select-dollar-params
items.sql: SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE id = $1 AND status = $2

items.name: postgresql-select-in-dollar-params
items.sql: SELECT id FROM users WHERE status IN ($1, $2, $3)

items.name: postgresql-select-between-dollar-params
items.sql: SELECT id FROM users WHERE age BETWEEN $1 AND $2

items.name: postgresql-select-not-in-dollar-params
items.sql: SELECT id FROM users WHERE status NOT IN ($1, $2)

items.name: postgresql-select-not-between-dollar-params
items.sql: SELECT id FROM users WHERE age NOT BETWEEN $1 AND $2

items.name: postgresql-select-limit-dollar-params
items.sql: SELECT id FROM users WHERE name LIKE $1 ORDER BY id LIMIT $2 OFFSET $3

items.name: postgresql-insert-dollar-params
items.sql: INSERT INTO users (id, name, status) VALUES ($1, $2, $3)

items.name: postgresql-insert-multi-row-dollar-params
items.sql: INSERT INTO users (id, name) VALUES ($1, $2), ($3, $4)

items.name: postgresql-update-dollar-params
items.sql: UPDATE users SET name = $1, status = $2 WHERE id = $3

items.name: postgresql-delete-dollar-params
items.sql: DELETE FROM users WHERE id = $1 AND status = $2

items.name: postgresql-prepare-insert
items.sql: PREPARE insert_user(int, text) AS INSERT INTO users (id, name) VALUES ($1, $2)

items.name: postgresql-prepare-update
items.sql: PREPARE update_user(text, int) AS UPDATE users SET name = $1 WHERE id = $2

items.name: postgresql-prepare-delete
items.sql: PREPARE delete_user(int) AS DELETE FROM users WHERE id = $1

items.name: postgresql-execute-prepared-with-args
items.sql: EXECUTE insert_user(1, 'bob')

items.name: postgresql-deallocate-all
items.sql: DEALLOCATE ALL

items.name: postgresql-view-direct-column
items.sql: SELECT name FROM users

items.name: postgresql-view-star-qualified-star
items.sql: SELECT u.*, u.id FROM public.users u

items.name: postgresql-view-functions-and-args
items.sql: SELECT CONCAT(UPPER(first_name), last_name), COALESCE(nickname, name), CAST(age AS text) FROM users

items.name: postgresql-view-distinct-nested-functions
items.sql: SELECT DISTINCT LOW(UPPER(name)) FROM table1

items.name: postgresql-view-expressions-and-case
items.sql: SELECT first_name || last_name, UPPER(name) || '_x', CASE WHEN state = 1 THEN name ELSE fallback_name END FROM users

items.name: postgresql-view-group-having-order
items.sql: SELECT dept, COUNT(id) FROM users GROUP BY dept HAVING COUNT(id) > 1 ORDER BY dept

items.name: postgresql-view-join-on
items.sql: SELECT u.name FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id WHERE o.status = 'paid'

items.name: postgresql-view-window-array-row-tests
items.sql: SELECT SUM(amount) OVER (PARTITION BY dept ORDER BY created_at), ARRAY[id, age], ROW(id, age), name IS NULL, active IS TRUE FROM users

items.name: postgresql-view-bind-values
items.sql: UPDATE servers SET ip = $1 WHERE id = $2

items.name: postgresql-view-not-like-bind
items.sql: SELECT id FROM users WHERE name NOT LIKE $1

items.name: postgresql-view-not-ilike-bind
items.sql: SELECT id FROM users WHERE name NOT ILIKE $1

items.name: postgresql-view-not-similar-bind
items.sql: SELECT id FROM users WHERE name NOT SIMILAR TO $1

items.name: parse-error
items.sql: SELECT FROM
